author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

title: John 4.21,22

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus saith unto her, Woman, believe Me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye worship ye know not what; we know what we worship, for salvation is of the Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Everywhere, beloved, we have need of faith, faith the mother of blessings, the medicine of salvation; and without this it is impossible to possess any one of the great doctrines.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Without this, men are like to those who attempt to cross the open sea without a ship, who for a little way hold out by swimming, using both hands and feet, but when they have advanced farther, are quickly swamped by the waves: in like manner they who use their own reasonings, before they have learnt anything, suffer shipwreck; as also Paul saith, “Who concerning faith have made shipwreck.” ( 1 Tim. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That this be not our case, let us hold fast the sacred anchor by which Christ bringeth over the Samaritan woman now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when she had said, “How say ye that Jerusalem is the place in which men ought to worship?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ replied, “Believe Me, woman, that the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in Jerusalem, nor yet in this mountain, worship the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: An exceedingly great doctrine He revealed to her, and one which He did not mention either to Nicodemus or Nathanael.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: She was eager to prove her own privileges more honorable than those of the Jews; and this she subtly argued from the Fathers, but Christ met not this question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was for the time distracting to speak on the matter, and to show why the Fathers worshiped in the mountain, and why the Jews at Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore on this point He was silent, and having taken away from both places priority in dignity, rouses her soul by showing that neither Jews nor Samaritans possessed anything great in comparison with that which was to be given; and then He introduceth the difference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet even thus He declared that the Jews were more honorable, not preferring place to place, but giving them the precedence because of their intention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “About the ‘place’ of worship ye have no need henceforth to dispute, but in the ‘manner’ the Jews have an advantage over you Samaritans, for ‘ye,’ He saith, ‘worship ye know not what; we know what we worship.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then did the Samaritans “know not” what they worshiped?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they thought that God was local and partial; so at least they served Him, and so they sent to the Persians, and reported that “the God of this place is wroth with us” ( 2 Kings xxvi.), in this respect forming no higher opinion of Him than of their idols.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore they continued to serve both Him and devils, joining things which ought not to be joined.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews, on the contrary, were free from this supposition, at least the greater part of them, and knew that He was God of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “Ye worship ye know not what; we know what we worship.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do not wonder that He numbereth Himself among Jews, for He speaketh to the woman’s opinion of Him as though He were a Jewish Prophet, and therefore He putteth, “we worship.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that He is of the objects of worship is clear to every one, because to worship belongs to the creature, but to be worshiped to the Lord of the creature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But for a time He speaketh as a Jew; and the expression “we” in this place meaneth “we Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having then exalted what was Jewish, He next maketh Himself credible, and persuadeth the woman to give the greater heed to His words, by rendering His discourse above suspicion, and showing that He doth not exalt what belongs to them by reason of relationship to those of His own tribe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is clear, that one who had made these declarations concerning the place on which the Jews most prided themselves, and thought that they were superior to all, and who had taken away their high claims, would not after this speak to get favor of any, but with truth and prophetic power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore He had for a while removed her from such reasonings, say ing, “Woman, believe Me,” and what follows, then He addeth, “for salvation is of the Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith is of this kind: neither, that blessings to the world came from them, (for to know God and condemn idols had its beginning from them, and with you the very act of worship, although ye do it not rightly, yet received its origin from them,) or else, He speaketh of His own Coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather, one would not be wrong in calling both these things “salvation” which He said was “of the Jews”; which Paul implied when he said, “Of whom is Christ according to the flesh, who is God over all.” ( Rom. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He commendeth the old Covenant, and showeth that it is the root of blessings, and that He is throughout not opposed to the Law, since He maketh the groundwork of all good things to come from the Jews?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshipers shall worship the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We, O woman,” He saith, “excel you in the manner of our worship, but even this shall henceforth have an end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not the places only, but even the manner of serving God shall be changed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this change is at your very doors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: ‘For the hour cometh, and now is.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since what the Prophets said they said long before the event, to show that here it is not so, He saith, “And now is.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Think not, He saith, that this is a prophecy of such a kind as shall be accomplished after a long time, the fulfillment is already at hand and at your very doors, “when the true worshipers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In saying “true,” He excludeth Jews as well as Samaritans; for although the Jews be better than the Samaritans, yet are they far inferior to those that shall come, as inferior as is the type to the reality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He speaketh of the Church, that she is the “true” worship, and such as is meet for God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For the Father seeketh such to worship Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then He in times past sought such as these, He allowed to those others their way of worship, not willingly, but from condescension, and for this reason, that He might bring them in also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who then are “the true worshipers”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those who confine not their service by place, and who serve God in spirit; as Paul saith, “Whom I serve in my spirit in the Gospel of His Son”: and again, “I beseech you that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, acceptable unto God, your reasonable service.” ( Rom. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when he saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “God is a Spirit” [God is spirit].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He declareth nothing else than His incorporeal Nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now the service of that which is incorporeal must needs be of the same character, and must be offered by that in us which is incorporeal, to wit, the soul, and purity of mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith, “they that worship Him, must worship Him in spirit and in truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For because both Samaritans and Jews were careless about the soul, but took great pains about the body, cleansing it in divers ways, it is not, He saith, by purity of body, but by that which is incorporeal in us, namely the mind, that the incorporeal One is served.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Sacrifice then not sheep and calves, but dedicate thyself to the Lord; make thyself a holocaust, this is to offer a living sacrifice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye must worship “in truth” ; as former things were types, such as circumcision, and whole burnt offerings, and victims, and incense, they now no longer exist, but all is “truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a man must now circumcise not his flesh, but his evil thoughts, and crucify himself, and remove and slay his unreasonable desires.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The woman was made dizzy by His discourse, and fainted in at the sublimity of what He said, and, in her trouble, hear what she saith:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I know that Messias cometh, which is called Christ: when He is come, He will tell us all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus saith unto her, I am that speak unto thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And whence came the Samaritans to expect the coming of Christ, seeing that they received Moses only?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From the writings of Moses themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even in the beginning He revealed the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Let Us make man in Our Image, after Our Likeness” ( Gen. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 26 ), was said to the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was He who talked with Abraham in the tent. ( Gen. xviii.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Jacob prophesying concerning Him said, “A ruler shall not fail from Judah, nor a leader from his thighs, until He come for whom it is reserved, and He is the expectation of nations.” ( Gen. xviii.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Moses himself saith, “The Lord thy God will raise up unto you a Prophet of your brethren like unto me, unto Him shall ye hearken.” ( Deut. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the circumstances attending the serpent, and the rod of Moses, and Isaac, and the sheep, and many other things they who chose might select as proclaiming His coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And why, pray,” saith one, “did not Christ lead on the woman by these means? why did He instance the serpent to Nicodemus, and mention prophecy to Nathanael, but to her say nothing of the kind?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what reason, and why?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they were men, and were versed in these things, she a poor ignorant woman unpracticed in the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He doth not speak to her from them, but draweth her on by the “water” and by prophecy, and bringeth her to make mention of Christ and then revealeth Himself; which had He at first told the woman when she had not questioned Him, He would have seemed to her to trifle and talk idly, while as it is by bringing her little by little to mention Him, at a fitting time He revealed Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To the Jews, who continually said, “How long dost Thou make us to doubt? tell us if Thou art the Christ” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 24 ), to them He gave no clear answer, but to this woman He said plainly, that He is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the woman was more fair-minded than the Jews; they did not enquire to learn, but always to mock at Him, for had they desired to learn, the teaching which was by His words, and by the Scriptures, and by His miracles would have been sufficient.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The woman, on the contrary, said what she said from an impartial judgment and a simple mind, as is plain from what she did afterwards; for she both heard and believed, and netted others also, and in every circumstance we may observe the carefulness and faith of the woman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And upon this came His disciples,” (very seasonably did they come when the teaching was finished,) “and marveled that He talked with the woman, yet no man said, What seekest Thou? or, Why talkest Thou with her?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At what did they marvel?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At His want of pride and exceeding humility, that looked upon as He was, He endured with such lowliness of heart to talk with a woman poor, and a Samaritan.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still in their amazement they did not ask Him the reason, so well were they taught to keep the station of disciples, so much did they fear and reverence Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For although they did not as yet hold the right opinion concerning Him, still they gave heed unto Him as to some marvelous one, and paid Him much respect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet they frequently are seen to act confidently; as when John lay upon His bosom, when they came to Him and said, “Who is the greatest in the Kingdom of Heaven?” ( Matt. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ), when the sons of Zebedee entreated Him to set one of them on His right hand, and the other on His left.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then did they not here question Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because since all those instances related to themselves, they had need to enquire into them, while what here took place was of no such great importance to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And indeed John did that a long time after towards the very end, when He enjoyed greater confidence, and was bold in the love of Christ; for he it was, he saith, “whom Jesus loved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What could equal such blessedness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But, beloved, let us not stop at this, the calling the Apostle blessed, but let us do all things that we also may be of the blessed, let us imitate the Evangelist, and see what it was that caused such great love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then was it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He left his father, his ship, and his net, and followed Jesus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet this he did in common with his brother, and Peter, and Andrew, and the rest of the Apostles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then was the special thing which caused this great love?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Shall we discover it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith nothing of this kind about himself, but only that he was beloved; as to the righteous acts for which he was beloved he has modestly been silent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That Jesus loved him with an especial love was clear to every one; yet John doth not appear conversing with or questioning Jesus privately, as Peter often did, and Philip, and Judas, and Thomas, except only when he desired to show kindness and compliance to his fellow Apostle; for when the chief of the Apostles by beckoning constrained him, then he asked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For these two had great love each for the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus, for instance, they are seen going up together into the Temple and speaking in common to the people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet Peter in many places is moved, and speaks more warmly than John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And at the end he hears Christ say, “Peter, lovest thou Me more than these?” ( c. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now it is clear that he who loved “more than these” was also beloved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this in his case was shown by loving Jesus, in the case of the other by being beloved by Jesus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then was it which caused this especial love?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To my thinking, it was that the man displayed great gentleness and meekness, for which reason he doth not appear in many places speaking openly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how great a thing this is, is plain also from the case of Moses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was this which made him such and so great as he was.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There is nothing equal to lowliness of mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For which cause Jesus with this began the Beatitudes, and when about to lay as it were the foundation and base of a mighty building, He placed first lowliness of mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Without this a man cannot possibly be saved; though he fast, though he pray, though he give alms, if it be with a proud spirit, these things are abominable, if humility be not there; while if it be, all these things are amiable and lovely, and are done with safety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then be modest, beloved, let us be modest; success is easy, if we be sober-minded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For after all what is it, O man, that exciteth thee to pride?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou not the poverty of thy nature? the unsteadiness of thy will?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider thine end, consider the multitude of thy sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps because thou doest many righteous deeds thou art proud.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By that very pride thou shalt undo them all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore it behoveth not so much him that has sinned as him that doeth righteousness to take pains to be humble.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because the sinner is constrained by conscience, while the other, except he be very sober, soon caught up as by a blast of wind is lifted on high, and made to vanish like the Pharisee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou give to the poor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What thou givest is not thine, but thy Master’s, common to thee and thy fellow-servants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For which cause thou oughtest especially to be humbled, in the calamities of those who are thy kindred foreseeing thine own, and taking knowledge of thine own nature in their cases.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We ourselves perhaps are sprung from such ancestors; and if wealth has shifted to you, it is probable that it will leave you again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And after all, what is wealth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A vain shadow, dissolving smoke, a flower of the grass, or rather something meaner than a flower.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then art thou high-minded over grass?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Doth not wealth fall to thieves, and effeminates, and harlots, and tomb-breakers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Doth this puff thee up, that thou hast such as these to share in thy possession? or dost thou desire honor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Towards gaining honor nothing is more serviceable than almsgiving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the honors arising from wealth and power are compulsory, and attended with hatred, but these others are from the free will and real feeling of the honorers; and therefore those who pay them can never give them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if men show such reverence for the merciful, and invoke all blessings upon them, consider what return, what recompense they shall receive from the merciful God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then seek this wealth which endureth forever, and never deserts us, that, becoming great here and glorious there, we may obtain everlasting blessings, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy Spirit be glory, now and ever, and world without end.
